verses.text: 'My son, forget not my law,' for keeping it brings length of days and peace; bind mercy and truth about thee, and so find 'favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.' Obedience is rewarded with favour. A heart bound to God's word commends a man to heaven and earth alike.

'My son, forget not my law,' for keeping it brings length of days and peace; bind mercy and truth about thee, and so find 'favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.' Obedience is rewarded with favour. A heart bound to God's word commends a man to heaven and earth alike.

'My son, forget not my law,' for keeping it brings length of days and peace; bind mercy and truth about thee, and so find 'favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.' Obedience is rewarded with favour. A heart bound to God's word commends a man to heaven and earth alike.

'My son, forget not my law,' for keeping it brings length of days and peace; bind mercy and truth about thee, and so find 'favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.' Obedience is rewarded with favour. A heart bound to God's word commends a man to heaven and earth alike.

'Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding'; acknowledge Him in all thy ways, and He shall direct thy paths. Self-distrust and God-reliance go together. To submit our way to God is the surest guidance.

'Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding'; acknowledge Him in all thy ways, and He shall direct thy paths. Self-distrust and God-reliance go together. To submit our way to God is the surest guidance.

'Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding'; acknowledge Him in all thy ways, and He shall direct thy paths. Self-distrust and God-reliance go together. To submit our way to God is the surest guidance.

'Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding'; acknowledge Him in all thy ways, and He shall direct thy paths. Self-distrust and God-reliance go together. To submit our way to God is the surest guidance.

'Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits,' and the barns shall be filled with plenty. Giving God the first is honoured with increase. He is no man's debtor who gives Him the best.

'Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits,' and the barns shall be filled with plenty. Giving God the first is honoured with increase. He is no man's debtor who gives Him the best.

Despise not the LORD's chastening nor faint under it, 'for whom the LORD loveth he correcteth,' as a father his son. Discipline is a mark of love. God's corrections are the proof, not the denial, of His fatherly care.

Despise not the LORD's chastening nor faint under it, 'for whom the LORD loveth he correcteth,' as a father his son. Discipline is a mark of love. God's corrections are the proof, not the denial, of His fatherly care.

Happy is the man who finds wisdom, for her gain is better than silver, gold, or rubies; 'nothing thou canst desire is to be compared unto her.' Wisdom is the supreme treasure. No earthly wealth can match the value of knowing how to live before God.

Happy is the man who finds wisdom, for her gain is better than silver, gold, or rubies; 'nothing thou canst desire is to be compared unto her.' Wisdom is the supreme treasure. No earthly wealth can match the value of knowing how to live before God.

Happy is the man who finds wisdom, for her gain is better than silver, gold, or rubies; 'nothing thou canst desire is to be compared unto her.' Wisdom is the supreme treasure. No earthly wealth can match the value of knowing how to live before God.

In her hands are length of days, riches, and honour; her ways are pleasantness and peace, and 'she is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her.' Wisdom blesses the whole of life. To grasp her is to find both sweetness and lasting good.

In her hands are length of days, riches, and honour; her ways are pleasantness and peace, and 'she is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her.' Wisdom blesses the whole of life. To grasp her is to find both sweetness and lasting good.

In her hands are length of days, riches, and honour; her ways are pleasantness and peace, and 'she is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her.' Wisdom blesses the whole of life. To grasp her is to find both sweetness and lasting good.

'The LORD by wisdom hath founded the earth,' establishing the heavens and the deeps by His knowledge. The world itself is built by wisdom. The same wisdom that made all things is offered to guide our lives.

'The LORD by wisdom hath founded the earth,' establishing the heavens and the deeps by His knowledge. The world itself is built by wisdom. The same wisdom that made all things is offered to guide our lives.

Keep sound wisdom and discretion, and they shall be life and grace; then thou shalt walk safely, and 'thy sleep shall be sweet.' Wisdom brings security and rest. The mind kept by God's truth lies down without fear.

Keep sound wisdom and discretion, and they shall be life and grace; then thou shalt walk safely, and 'thy sleep shall be sweet.' Wisdom brings security and rest. The mind kept by God's truth lies down without fear.

Keep sound wisdom and discretion, and they shall be life and grace; then thou shalt walk safely, and 'thy sleep shall be sweet.' Wisdom brings security and rest. The mind kept by God's truth lies down without fear.

Keep sound wisdom and discretion, and they shall be life and grace; then thou shalt walk safely, and 'thy sleep shall be sweet.' Wisdom brings security and rest. The mind kept by God's truth lies down without fear.

Be not afraid of sudden fear nor of the ruin of the wicked, 'for the LORD shall be thy confidence.' God Himself is the believer's courage. Resting in Him, we need not dread the calamities that overtake others.

Be not afraid of sudden fear nor of the ruin of the wicked, 'for the LORD shall be thy confidence.' God Himself is the believer's courage. Resting in Him, we need not dread the calamities that overtake others.

Withhold not good from those to whom it is due, nor put off a neighbour when you can help today; devise no evil against him who dwells trustingly by you. Wisdom is just and kind. To be ready with good and slow to wrong our neighbour is true godliness.

Withhold not good from those to whom it is due, nor put off a neighbour when you can help today; devise no evil against him who dwells trustingly by you. Wisdom is just and kind. To be ready with good and slow to wrong our neighbour is true godliness.

Withhold not good from those to whom it is due, nor put off a neighbour when you can help today; devise no evil against him who dwells trustingly by you. Wisdom is just and kind. To be ready with good and slow to wrong our neighbour is true godliness.

Strive not without cause, envy not the oppressor, choose none of his ways, for 'the froward is abomination to the LORD,' but His secret is with the righteous. God hates crookedness and befriends the upright. The path of peace and honesty is His delight.

Strive not without cause, envy not the oppressor, choose none of his ways, for 'the froward is abomination to the LORD,' but His secret is with the righteous. God hates crookedness and befriends the upright. The path of peace and honesty is His delight.

Strive not without cause, envy not the oppressor, choose none of his ways, for 'the froward is abomination to the LORD,' but His secret is with the righteous. God hates crookedness and befriends the upright. The path of peace and honesty is His delight.

The curse of the LORD is on the wicked's house, but He blesses the dwelling of the just; He gives grace to the lowly and shame to fools. Two households, two destinies. God scorns the scornful but lifts up the humble to honour.

The curse of the LORD is on the wicked's house, but He blesses the dwelling of the just; He gives grace to the lowly and shame to fools. Two households, two destinies. God scorns the scornful but lifts up the humble to honour.
